info.title: Create generic Spoon rule for logback-core API breakage

parts.text: "Project @singer/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @singer/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/logback-core-1.2.9-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-logback-core-1.2.9/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @singer/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Evaluating project tasks**

I realize I need to inspect the repository and it seems likely I should use a TODO approach since things are complex. I might need to update the commentary before using any tools. Maybe exploring a task could help, although I feel like I’m lacking the skills. I think I’ll need to use commands like glob, grep, or read, and perhaps apply patches. It’s crucial to inspect the structure too.
I’m locating the affected source, the template project, and the dependency docs first so I can derive a reusable API-level rewrite rather than patching `@singer/` directly.

91:    * @param context the logback context.
92:    */
93:   public static Appender<LogMessage> createFileRollingThriftAppender(
94:       File basePath,
95:       String topic,
96:       long rotateThresholdKBytes,
97:       Context context,
98:       int maxRetentionHours) {
99:     RollingFileAppender<LogMessage> appender = new RollingFileAppender<LogMessage>();
100:     appender.setContext(context);
101:     appender.setAppend(true);
102:     appender.setPrudent(false);
103: 
104:     LogMessageEncoder encoder = new LogMessageEncoder();
105:     appender.setEncoder(encoder);
106:     appender.setFile(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic);
107: 
108:     TimeBasedRollingPolicy policy = new TimeBasedRollingPolicy();
109:     policy.setMaxHistory(maxRetentionHours);
110:     policy.setFileNamePattern(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic + ".%d{yyyy-MM-dd-HH}.%i");
111:     policy.setCleanHistoryOnStart(false);
112:     policy.setContext(context);
113:     policy.setParent(appender);
114: 
115:     // Also impose a max size per file policy.
116:     SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP fnatp = new SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP();
117:     fnatp.setContext(context);
118:     fnatp.setTimeBasedRollingPolicy(policy);
119:     fnatp.setMaxFileSize(FileSize.valueOf(String.format("%sKB", rotateThresholdKBytes)));
120: 
121:     policy.setTimeBasedFileNamingAndTriggeringPolicy(fnatp);
122:     appender.setRollingPolicy(policy);
123:     appender.setTriggeringPolicy(policy);
124: 
125:     policy.start();
126:     appender.start();
127:

101:         RollingFileAppender<LogMessage> appender = new RollingFileAppender<LogMessage>();
102:         appender.setContext(context);
103:         appender.setAppend(true);
104:         appender.setPrudent(false);
105:         LogMessageEncoder encoder = new LogMessageEncoder();
106:         appender.setEncoder(encoder);
107:         appender.setFile((basePath + PATH_SEP) + topic);
108:         TimeBasedRollingPolicy policy = new TimeBasedRollingPolicy();
109:         policy.setMaxHistory(maxRetentionHours);
110:         policy.setFileNamePattern(((basePath + PATH_SEP) + topic) + ".%d{yyyy-MM-dd-HH}.%i");
111:         policy.setCleanHistoryOnStart(false);
112:         policy.setContext(context);
113:         policy.setParent(appender);
114:         // Also impose a max size per file policy.
115:         SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP fnatp = new SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP();
116:         fnatp.setContext(context);
117:         fnatp.setTimeBasedRollingPolicy(policy);
118:         fnatp.setMaxFileSize(FileSize.valueOf(String.format("%sKB", rotateThresholdKBytes)));
119:         policy.setTimeBasedFileNamingAndTriggeringPolicy(fnatp);
120:         appender.setRollingPolicy(policy);
121:         appender.setTriggeringPolicy(policy);

44:     try {
45:       String hostName = InetAddress.getLocalHost().getHostName();
46:       int firstDotPos = hostName.indexOf('.');
47:       if (firstDotPos > 0) {
48:         HOST_NAME = hostName.substring(0, firstDotPos);
49:       } else {
50:         HOST_NAME = hostName;
51:       }
52:     } catch (Exception e) {
53:       // fall back to env var.

78:     try {
79:       if (partitionKey != null) {
80:         logMessage.setKey(partitionKey);
81:       }

83:       OpenTsdbMetricConverter.incr(

87:           THRIFT_LOGGER_ERROR_LOGBACKEXCEPTION, "topic=" + topic, "host=" + HOST_NAME);
88:       throw e;
89:     }
90:   }
91: 
92:   @Override

102:       append(partitionKey, messageBytes, timeNanos);

state.title: Rebuild singer after exception fix

parts.text:
Implemented a generic Spoon rewrite in `/workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` and validated it against `@singer/`.

What it does:
- Targets any `CtClass` subtype of `ch.qos.logback.core.encoder.EncoderBase`
- Renames legacy `doEncode(...)` to `encode(...)`
- Removes stale `@Override` annotations from `init` and `close`
- Adds missing `headerBytes()` and `footerBytes()` methods
- Uses only dependency API types, not project-specific identifiers

Validation:
- `mvn -q test -DskipTests` in `spoon-base-template` passed
- Ran the transformation against `@singer/`
- `mvn -q -pl thrift-logger -am test -DskipITs` in `singer` passed

Abstract breaking change:
- Old pattern: `EncoderBase` subclass with `doEncode(E)` plus `init(OutputStream)`/`close()`
- New pattern: `Encoder<E>` requires `encode(E)`, `headerBytes()`, and `footerBytes()`
- Required structural fix: migrate the encoding method to `encode`, drop invalid override annotations, and provide the new byte-array hooks
